title: lotus corniculatus subsp. glacialis
intro: Lotus corniculatus subsp. glacialis, also known as the alpine lotus or glacialis bird's-foot-trefoil, is a perennial plant belonging to the pea family (Fabaceae). Traditionally, this plant has been used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antibacterial effects, which are attributed to its high content of flavonoids and saponins. The health benefits of lotus corniculatus subsp. glacialis include reducing inflammation, improving wound healing, and exhibiting antioxidant properties, which can help protect against oxidative stress and cell damage. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures, and can also be found in dried or fresh form for use in salads or as a garnish.
